original_hypothesis.title: Royalty payout burn via approving default zero-address proposals (fund loss) despite 'approved' status
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The royalties approval gate can be satisfied even when no payout addresses were ever proposed: acceptAddressesAndPercentages() can set status=true unconditionally. payArtist() then sends ETH to primaryAdd1/2/3 which may all be address(0), effectively burning the artist share. This is a concrete code-path fund loss, triggered by a privileged workflow error or malicious function-admin.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Operationally realistic during incidents or misconfiguration: a function admin can mistakenly 'accept' without verifying proposals. The resulting loss is irreversible on-chain.

proof_of_signal.pattern: acceptAddressesAndPercentages sets status flags without checking addresses != 0 or that a proposal exists; payArtist uses .call to those addresses

preconditions: Function-admin privileges to call acceptAddressesAndPercentages/payArtist (or operational mistake by authorized party)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Admin approval does not bind to artist proposal existence; status can be set true independently of any address configuration.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Yes (requires privileged misuse), but the loss is due to missing validation in code.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time prior to payout for a funded collection

operational_questions: Are there operational runbooks preventing acceptance without proposals, or is this a common admin action?
Does payArtist include any off-chain verification step or on-chain checks of non-zero addresses?
Can emergencyWithdraw be abused to 'recover' burnt shares, and who receives it?
validation_requirements: Test: call acceptAddressesAndPercentages(true,...) on fresh collection; call payArtist and confirm calls to address(0)
Check whether .call to address(0) succeeds (it typically does) and burns funds

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Accept statuses without setting proposal addresses.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Call payArtist to trigger ETH sends to zero addresses.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Optionally use emergencyWithdraw to drain remaining balances (if any).

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Artist/team payments fail irreversibly; reputational damage.
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Royalty payout operations for any funded collection

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: PayArtist events where recipient is 0x0; unusual acceptance events (note: accept has no event).
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Artist complaints of non-receipt despite payArtist executed.
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Collections with splits set but no proposals followed by acceptance.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
